reviews.title: Bose and Best Buy service
reviews.text: Bose speaker sounds good, but this will be the 3rd one of this model Luckily I purchased the warranty each time because right around 1 1/2 years the speaker gets to a point it either doesn't power down or the bluetooth no longer works or both. I am not buying the extended warranty this time around because thats almost $200 each time and if it does it again I will look at a different brand. Now about Best Buy service. It Sucks! Sorry, just being honest. I bought a replacement warranty, but this model in Black is sold only on the Best Buy website  and it's it's from a 3rd party vendor so I had to pay another $30 after tax to get it replaced. If I would've got the gray model it would have been a straight trade. I had a Black one and I wanted a Black one, plain and simple.  It's available on their website so if there was an additional cost I would think they should've paid it. I bought it, they offered it so done deal, but NO, I had to pay it. Not very good service since thats not how you advertise your replacement warranty when you sell it.  And when they sold it to me I was supposed to get it in two days and it's been over two weeks and I still don't have it. All it been saying on the website is it's delayed with no update. This sucks because I used that speaker everyday when I'm getting ready.  I feel for the price I can't recommend this product. Should last a lot longer. Like I said in four years I'm on my 3rd one.

reviews.author: MiaF

reviews.title: Backed by Bose’s quality standards
reviews.text:
This Bose speaker has a wonderful sound. It delivers crystal-clear, deep bass and highs that are sharp and easy to hear without losing quality. The 360° sound effect makes whatever you’re listening to feel like it surrounds you, rather than coming from just one direction, which creates a more immersive audio experience, all with the Bose seal of quality.

The only reason it doesn’t earn five stars is the Alexa connection. Sometimes you have to reset the speaker to factory settings in order to reconnect it to your Alexa account, which can be very tedious. Because of this, I often just use it with Bluetooth instead. Other than that, I love the speaker for what it is, and the battery actually lasts quite a long time—as long as you remember to turn it off when you’re not using it.

reviews.author: MarkJ

reviews.title: This is NOT its predecessor!
reviews.text: I have the revolve first generation that is more than 12 years old and has had zero issues. The battery still on high volume is 4 to 5 hours even after all this time. This new product is NOT the same, it has had consistent issues with incredibly short battery time even when "fully turned off". Audio books at 3/4 volume kill the battery in less than 5 hours and music of any type at mid to low volumes kill the battery in less than 3.  It is constantly listening regardless of status, it gets stuck in boot loops for sometimes 15 mins, there is no hard reset, zero app support and I have never ever gotten the app to work properly on my android. I will be returning as it just killed a 100% charge battery in less than 2 hours with an audio book. I gave this a year to sort itself out or just deal with it being a typically high priced piece of garbage and I have had it with dieing in the middle of listening with no warning.

This expensive high quality speaker now functions as an effective door stop.

reviews.title: Maybe it is good but it could be better!
reviews.text: I initially have issues connecting the bluetooth/wireless feature.  I attempted several times before I finally figured it out.  One may assume it was user error but speaking frankly, the instructions are not crystal clear and if you are not tech savvy, then you may have issues as I did.  Thereafter, it's okay.  The volume isn't what I thought it would be and you have to choose either Google Assistant or Alexa.  They both cannot be loaded at the same time....at least that is what I found to be true.  But again, I'm not tech savvy but if they are, then that means the directions to do so are not crystal clear!  And finally, ....there isn't a remote to control the volume!  All in all, I would not recommend this product based off price.

reviews.title: Amazing Sound and Convenience!
reviews.text:
The Bose Portable Smart Speaker is absolutely phenomenal! The sound quality is incredible for such a compact speaker – deep bass, clear mids, and crisp highs. Whether I'm listening to music, podcasts, or taking a call, the audio is always rich and immersive. The built-in WiFi and Bluetooth make it super easy to connect to my devices, and I love the option to switch between Google Assistant and Alexa voice control. The speaker responds quickly to voice commands, and controlling my music or smart home devices is effortless.

The portability is another huge plus – it’s lightweight and easy to move around the house or take outside. The battery life is impressive, lasting for hours of continuous play, and the speaker charges quickly when needed. The sleek, modern design in Triple Black looks great in any setting.

Overall, the Bose Portable Smart Speaker is the perfect combination of superior sound, convenience, and smart functionality. I couldn’t be happier with my purchase and would highly recommend it to anyone looking for a top-tier portable speaker!

reviews.author: Luis

reviews.title: Powerful Sound in a Compact Design! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
reviews.text:
I recently got the Bose Portable Smart Speaker in Triple Black, and it has exceeded my expectations! The sound quality is absolutely crisp, rich, and room-filling, making it perfect for any setting.

What I Love:
Amazing Sound Quality – Deep bass, clear vocals, and 360-degree audio make music and podcasts sound incredible.
 Portable & Lightweight – Easy to carry around the house or take outdoors.
WiFi & Bluetooth Connectivity – Streams effortlessly from multiple devices.
Voice Assistants Built-In – Alexa and Google Assistant work seamlessly for hands-free control.
 Long Battery Life – Lasts up to 12 hours on a single charge.

The sleek Triple Black finish looks premium, and the water-resistant design is a bonus for outdoor use. If you're looking for a high-quality portable smart speaker, this one is worth every penny!

reviews.title: Great portable sound in a small package!
reviews.text:
I love these "pails"!  We have 2 of them and got my brother-in-law one as a gift.
Setup of this and all of the Bose speakers I bought has been finicky at best.  Sometimes the speakers won't group, sometimes show as unavailable.  But when they work, it's really great.
And of course, it sounds like Bose...which I have been a fan for years. dating back to the old Lifestyle systems (which I still have 2 of them that sound great after 10+ years).  it's amazing how good they sound given their size.  I carry it around the house or out into the yard.  
I also bought the charging cradle to make it easy to just plop it down and let it charge.
Love these!

reviews.title: Bad experience
reviews.text: My first Bose was the BOSE SOUNDLINK REVOLVE+ II. Love it so much I bought second one for my father. Then purchased this model and was the worst mistake. Battery kept dying while being charged. I did purchase the protection plan but it was not fair to use. I sent for repair and took 3 weeks to comeback. It worked for like a month the. The same issue. Kept dying while charging. Total time of having speaker was for 14 months. I finally used my protection plan and my intention was to get it replaced. Well best buy gave me back the money I had payed for speaker which was $432. Store credit. I bought the BOSE SOUNDLINK REVOLVE+ II again for $229. I still had almost $200 left. Couldn’t be happier. Maybe I just had a defective speaker but that’s why it was sent for repair. Also forgot to mention I could never get the app to work with the speaker.

reviews.text: Bose seems to be trying to compete with the Sonos line or the HomePod. Its trails FAR behind both in audio performance. It gets plenty loud and has heaps of bass. It CAN operate on battery for a paltry 12 hours, which is an odd addition. Unless you’re ride-or-die for Bose, consider alternatives.
